item.title: Ryanair suspends guidance after surge in jet fuel price
item.description: Carrier confirms new contract negotiations with chief Michael O’Leary after annual profits rise by a third

item.title: Fears of Samsung strike ease after court ruling
item.description: Shares rise after injunction restricts unions’ ability to halt production at South Korean chipmaker

item.title: China is not Japan
item.description: But its real estate market has been doing a darn good impression

item.title: Private capital firms circle L&G
item.description: Plus, SpaceX’s rocketing value, Japan’s AI, and Zurbarán at The National Gallery

item.title: FirstFT: Mortgage costs jump on Iran war
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Labour’s Brexit debate and German unemployment

item.title: Anthropic to brief global financial watchdog on cyber flaws exposed by Mythos
item.description: US tech group will discuss capabilities of its new AI model with members of the Financial Stability Board

item.title: European oil refiners and airlines confident jet fuel shortages can be avoided
item.description: Refiners maximise production and increase imports from US and Africa to offset loss of Middle Eastern supplies

item.title: EU plans to force companies to buy parts from non-Chinese suppliers
item.description: New rules to target chemicals and industrial machinery makers

item.title: Defence groups clamour to delay US ban on Chinese rare earth magnets
item.description: Military contractors want more time to comply with prohibition years in the making and now just months away

item.title: Jim Ratcliffe’s Ineos takes €200mn punt on chemical sector peers
item.description: Group invests in basket of company equities and tells bondholders the sector is ‘undervalued’

item.title: UBS: the bank that outgrew a country
item.description: Switzerland says tougher capital rules are needed to guard against disaster. Executives disagree. Shareholders suggest relocation

item.title: The fate of OpenAI’s $1tn IPO will be decided in an Oakland jury room
item.description: Elon Musk’s legal challenge could derail the AI start-up’s commercial ambitions

item.title: Mortgage costs rise sharply on Middle East conflict
item.description: Home loans have become more expensive in North America and Europe despite central banks keeping rates on hold

item.title: Investment firms look beyond Iran war to expand in Middle East
item.description: Asset managers and hedge funds say they remain committed to the region despite the current disruption

item.title: Why luxury brands are courting tennis stars
item.description: The style of athletes and a shifting sportswear market are turning the game into the industry’s newest obsession

item.title: Sweeping the strait: the companies gearing up to clear the Gulf of mines
item.description: New generation of uncrewed vessels could help restore traffic in vital shipping route

item.title: Business schools move beyond the basics to teach collaboration with AI
item.description: Executive education increasingly focuses on decision-making amid shifting technological capabilities

item.title: Where does ambition go without direction?
item.description: In retirement, traditional markers of success become murkier and structure must come from yourself

item.title: Asia is headed towards an industrial supercycle
item.description: It’s not just AI — energy, defence and industrial capex will fuel growth

item.title: Publicis to buy US data company in $2.2bn deal as it deepens AI marketing push
item.description: French advertising group to purchase LiveRamp and enhance its focus on disruptive technology

item.title: The looming energy crunch
item.description: Supply squeeze due to the Iran war will get worse before it gets better

item.title: Iran energy crisis enters new phase as peak summer season looms
item.description: Emergency measures spread as oil stockpiles run low

item.title: UAE says drone strike caused fire at nuclear plant
item.description: Attack at Barakah plant caused no injuries, and authorities say radiation levels remain normal

item.title: In America, wealth taxes mean wealth flight
item.description: California’s intended targets, whether individuals or companies, are leaving for friendlier regimes

item.title: The future of America’s military industrial complex
item.description: In an age of disruption, defence is undergoing seismic and lucrative change

item.title: Donald Trump Jr’s VC firm flaunts political edge in investment push
item.description: 1789 Capital is seeking to grow its assets to $10bn after building sprawling AI portfolio

item.title: Deadly drone strikes on Moscow ‘entirely justified’, says Zelenskyy
item.description: Ukrainian president claims responsibility for attacks in retaliation for Russia’s bombardment of Kyiv

item.title: ‘Nervous energy’: US wind and solar projects at risk as tax credits expire
item.description: Developers face labour and equipment shortages amid hostility from the Trump administration

item.title: Bain Capital closes largest Asia fund after raising $10.5bn
item.description: Buyout firm raised $2.1bn more from external investors than had been targeted

item.title: How Britain can tackle its mountain of unpaid energy bills
item.description: A long-term plan such as a social tariff could stop vulnerable households from running up debt
